channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Western countries step up support for Ukraine as Russian and US envoys meet
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Britain on Friday announced a “surge” of military support to Ukraine, as the war-ravaged country’s Western backers gathered to drum up more weapons and ammunition to fight off Russia's invasion and a U.S. envoy flew to St. Petersburg amid ongoing ceasefire efforts.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 50 years later, Vietnam's battlefields draw retrospective veterans and other tourists
item.description: HO CHI MINH CITY, Vietnam (AP) — Hamburger Hill, Hue, the Ia Drang Valley, Khe Sanh: Some remember the Vietnam War battles from the headlines of the 1960s and 1970s, others from movies and history books.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and HAU DINH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Condemned SC man's case about 'appropriate punishment' as he awaits 'inhumane' firing squad execution: lawyer
item.description: A South Carolina man on death row prepares to be put to death by firing squad, as his lawyer says the case is a matter of "appropriate punishment" rather than guilt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Connecticut house of horrors stepmom denies child abuse accusations that came as ‘extreme shock’: attorney
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan, the Waterbury, Connecticut woman accused of abusing her stepson, denies the accusations that came as an "extreme shock," her attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE highlights arrests of 5 illegal aliens convicted of violent crimes, including against children
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement highlighted five arrests made by agents across the United States on Wednesday. All five people are convicted criminals and Mexican nationals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian opposition leader says his home was the target of an alleged bomb plot
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian opposition leader Peter Dutton confirmed on Friday that his family home had been the target of an alleged bomb plot, but said concerns for his personal safety did not restrict his election campaigning.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge will decide whether to proceed with Menendez brothers resentencing hearing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A judge is set to decide Friday whether to proceed with resentencing hearings for Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of murdering their parents, in light of Los Angeles' new district attorney opposing their release after 30 years behind bars.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In fight over insurance, neighbors crowdsource LA fire contamination data
item.description: Los Angeles-area residents whose homes survived the Eaton wildfire in January face a whole different set of struggles to their neighbors whose homes were burned to the ground.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Lauer And Sally Ho, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pro-Palestinian demonstrators who occupied Stanford building are charged with felonies
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — A dozen pro-Palestinian demonstrators who were arrested at Stanford University last year after they occupied and allegedly caused hundreds of thousands of dollars in damage to a campus building are now facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No, Casey Anthony did not open a Branson daycare
item.description: Google Trends showed a variety of topics being searched in Missouri on Thursday morning---sports, weather, the lottery, and one specific question: Did Casey Anthony open a daycare in Branson, Missouri?
item.dc:creator: Megan Mueller, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Masked anti-Israel Stanford protesters charged in destructive 2024 campus building takeover
item.description: A dozen pro-Palestinian protesters charged in connection to the June 2024 takeover and destruction of a building at Stanford University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she will prevent the Trump administration from ordering hundreds of thousands of Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans with temporary legal status to leave the country later this month.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Supreme Court tells Trump administration to facilitate return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said President Donald Trump’s administration must facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge dismisses charges in alleged campus vigilante 'Catch a Predator' sting targeting Army soldier
item.description: Five Massachusetts college students accused of attacking a man in a "To Catch a Predator" sting have had their kidnapping and conspiracy charges dropped.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maryland immigrant wrongly deported to El Salvador must return to US, Supreme Court rules
item.description: The Supreme Court has ordered the Federal Government to facilitate the return of Salvadoran national Kilmar Abrego Garcia, of Maryland, back to the United States after he was unlawfully deported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Suspected of backing a coup plot against the longtime dictator of Gambia nearly 20 years ago, Pharing Sanyang described Thursday how he was beaten with pipes and palm tree branches, pistol-whipped and hit in the face with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Witness captures video of helicopter breaking apart, crashing into Hudson River
item.description: A witness captured video of the moment a helicopter came crashing down into the Hudson River between Manhattan and New Jersey Thursday, killing six people.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie" around the country as they take part in the so-called "chicken jockey" trend.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DeSantis defends $10 million donation from state agency settlement to charity linked to his wife
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis on Thursday defended a state agency's involvement with a $10 million donation to a charity founded by his wife as a “cherry on top” deal using funds from a settlement between the agency and the state’s largest Medicaid contractor.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized state party in swing state is leaving
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Wisconsin Democratic leader who revitalized the party in the presidential swing state announced Thursday he will be leaving his position in June after a six-year run that included a string of victories and “agonizingly close” losses.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report: Drug lord linked to DEA agent’s death freed
item.description: One of the three leaders of the Guadalajara cartel linked to the torture-slaying of Drug Enforcement Administration Agent Enrique “Kiki” Camarena in 1985 has been released in Mexico, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Trump nominee for public lands post withdraws after her criticism of Jan. 6 attack surfaces
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to oversee an agency that manages a quarter-billion acres of public land has withdrawn her nomination following revelations that she criticized the Republican president in 2021 for inciting the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Foster mom accused of exchanging child for exotic animal in bizarre trade
item.description: Brenda Deutsch is facing multiple felony charges after Missouri prosecutors say she allegedly tried to trade a foster child in her care for an "exotic animal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnson scraps vote on Trump budget blueprint in face of conservative opposition
item.description: House Republican leaders on Wednesday scrapped plans to vote on the Senate’s framework to advance key parts of President Trump’s legislative agenda, a major setback that came in the face of opposition from hard-line conservatives. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Brawl erupts in Kansas courtroom during murder sentencing
item.description: The murder victim's mother spoke to the judge before the sentencing. Her words sparked anger among some of the other people in the courtroom.
item.dc:creator: Carina Branson, NewsNation

item.title: Woman can testify Weinstein used force to rape her despite his acquittal, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A woman who says Harvey Weinstein trapped her in a Manhattan hotel room and raped her in 2013 can use the word “force” when she testifies at his retrial even though the disgraced movie mogul was acquitted of using physical force against her, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Previous execution reveals how South Carolina will put 2nd inmate to death by firing squad
item.description: South Carolina is legally required to keep some protocols secret as it prepares to put a man to death by firing squad for the murder of a public safety officer, but the only previous execution by bullets the state carried out last month reveals a few key details.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp to 78 cents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Postal Service is seeking a rate increase this summer that includes hiking the cost of a first-class stamp from 73 cents to 78 cents. The request was made Wednesday to the Postal Regulatory Commission, which must OK the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Universal Studios announces plans to build first European theme park resort
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences officially announced on Tuesday that it intends to build its first-ever theme park and resort in the United Kingdom. In 2023, Universal purchased a 476-acre parcel of land outside Bedford, England, about 55 miles north of London.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Teen seen shoving guns through border wall into Mexico
item.description: An Arizona teen allegedly was caught slipping guns and knives through the border wall south into Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, Fox 5

item.title: Kidnapping and conspiracy charges are dropped against college students in ‘Catch a Predator’ fad
item.description: A judge has dismissed conspiracy and kidnapping charges against five Massachusetts college students who were accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on social media.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5
